# Senior QA / Test Engineer

Own quality across the product engineering team. You'll build the test
strategy, the automation, and the culture — not just hunt bugs.

## What you'll do

- Define the team's testing strategy across unit, integration, and
  end-to-end. Help engineers understand what each layer is for.
- Build and maintain the end-to-end test suite in Playwright. Keep it
  fast, deterministic, and trusted — a flaky test is worse than no test.
- Partner with backend engineers on API contract testing.
- Partner with frontend engineers on visual regression and accessibility
  testing.
- Drive the bug triage loop: severity, reproducibility, owner. Close the
  loop from production incidents back to test coverage.
- Participate in code review with a quality-first lens — not just
  "is this tested" but "is the test the right test."
- Improve CI/CD quality gates: PR checks, mainline health, release
  readiness.
- Take part in the on-call rotation; help drive postmortems toward
  systemic test improvements.

## What you bring

- 5+ years in test engineering or QA, with substantial automation work.
- Strong Playwright (or comparable end-to-end framework). You write tests
  other engineers can read and debug.
- Solid grasp of the testing pyramid — and when to break it.
- Comfortable in a production codebase. You can read the code under test,
  not just black-box it.
- API testing experience: contract tests, schema validation, fault
  injection.
- CI/CD discipline: you make the build trustworthy, you don't add tests
  that everyone learns to ignore.
- Communication: you can convince a tired engineer that a test isn't
  the bottleneck — bad code is.

## Stack

Playwright, Pytest, Postman/Newman, GitHub Actions, Datadog Synthetics,
TypeScript, Python.
